slides.title: What is a Trait? 🏅
slides.content:
A Trait simply tells the Rust compiler about functionality a particular type has and can share with other types.

If you know Java or C#, you can think of Traits exactly like **Interfaces**.

slides.title: Defining a Trait 📜
slides.content: You use the `trait` keyword and then list the method signatures (without the actual code block).

slides.title: Implementing a Trait 🛠️

quizData.question: What is the core purpose of a Trait in Rust?

options.text: To define exactly how much memory a struct uses
To strictly define a shared interface of behavior (methods) that multiple different struct types can implement.

quizData.explanation: Traits cleanly ensure multiple structs implement specific functionality securely.
